name: Portuguese Pastel de Nata
steps: Make a simple pastry from the flour and butter, and press into small tart tins.
Whisk the eggs, milk, and a little sugar together, and pour into the shells.
Bake at 425F for 18-22 minutes until the tops are deeply browned in spots.
steps_detailed: Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and a splash of cold water, and press into small tart tins, forming a thin shell.
Whisk the eggs, milk, sugar, and vanilla together until smooth.
Pour the custard into the shells.
Bake at 425F (220C) for 18-24 minutes until the custard is set and deeply browned in spots on top, mimicking the classic scorched-top look.
Dust with cinnamon before serving - Portugal's most famous export, the custard tart perfected at Lisbon's Belem monastery.

name: Portuguese Bread with Cheese and Ham
steps: Warm the ham briefly.
Layer the cheese and ham in the split bread.
Serve as is, or toast briefly for a melted version.
steps_detailed: Split the bread and butter the inside lightly.
Layer with the ham and cheese.
Toast in a pan over medium heat, pressing lightly, 2-3 minutes per side until the bread is golden and the cheese has melted - a simple, common Portuguese breakfast or light meal.

name: Portuguese Scrambled Eggs with Chourico

ingredients.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps: Brown the sliced sausage in the butter.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble gently until just set.
Serve hot with bread.

ingredients_full.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps_detailed: Saute the onion in half the butter until soft, then add the sliced sausage and brown it, 4-5 minutes, letting some of the paprika-red fat render out.
Beat the eggs with the black pepper and pour into the pan, stirring gently over low heat until just set and creamy.
Serve hot with crusty bread - chourico's smoky, paprika-forward flavor is central to Portuguese cooking.

name: Portuguese Caldo Verde

ingredients.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps: Simmer the potato in water until very tender, about 15 minutes, then mash into the broth to thicken.
Add the shredded cabbage and sliced sausage, and simmer 8-10 minutes until the cabbage is just tender.
Drizzle with the oil before serving.

ingredients_full.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps_detailed: Simmer the potato, onion, and garlic in 2.5 cups water for 15-18 minutes until the potato is very tender.
Blend or mash the mixture directly in the pot until smooth, creating the traditional silky green soup base.
Add the very thinly sliced cabbage (traditionally couve galega, a collard-like green) and the sliced sausage, and simmer 8-10 minutes until the cabbage is just tender but still has a little bite.
Drizzle with the oil and serve hot - Portugal's beloved national soup.

name: Portuguese Bacalhau a Bras
steps: Salt the fish generously and let sit 15 minutes, then poach or pan-fry until just cooked, and flake it.
Fry the potato until golden and crisp, then saute the onion until soft.
Combine the flaked fish, fried potato, and onion, then pour in the beaten eggs and stir gently until just set, like a soft scramble.
steps_detailed: Salt the fish generously and let it sit 15-20 minutes to firm up and concentrate flavor, mimicking salted, dried bacalhau.
Poach or steam the fish until just cooked through, then flake it into large pieces.
Cut the potato into thin matchstick fries and fry in the oil until golden and crisp; drain.
Saute the onion and garlic in a little of the oil until soft and golden, 6-8 minutes.

Combine the flaked fish, fried potato, and sauteed onion in the pan, then pour in the beaten eggs and stir gently over low heat until just set, keeping it soft and a little creamy, not fully scrambled.
Top with the sliced olives - one of Portugal's most beloved bacalhau (salt cod) dishes.

name: Portuguese Feijoada

ingredients.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps: Brown the pork and sausage together.
Add the beans and tomatoes with a splash of their liquid, and simmer 25-30 minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

ingredients_full.note: 1 serving, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps_detailed: Brown the pork and sliced sausage with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the beans, diced tomatoes, bay leaf, and oregano, along with a splash of the bean liquid.
Simmer 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pork is tender and the stew has thickened.
Remove the bay leaf and serve hot with rice - Portugal's hearty bean and pork stew, a dish shared in variation across the former Portuguese empire, including Brazil.

name: Portuguese Cozido

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef and sausage in water 40-45 minutes until the beef is tender.
Add the cabbage and carrot, and simmer 15-18 more minutes until tender.
Serve everything together with the broth.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Combine the beef, sausage, onion, and 3 cups water in a large pot. Simmer 40-50 minutes until the beef is tender.
Add the potato and carrot, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the cabbage and continue simmering 10-12 minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Serve everything together in wide bowls with the flavorful broth - Portugal's version of the boiled dinner found across the Iberian peninsula, traditionally made with many different meats.

name: Portuguese Piri Piri Chicken

ingredients.note: or piri piri pepper if available

steps: Blend the jalapeno, garlic, and lime juice with a little oil into a marinade.
Coat the chicken and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Grill or roast until well charred and cooked through, about 20-25 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: or piri piri pepper if available

steps_detailed: Blend the jalapeno, garlic, lime juice, oil, and oregano into a smooth, fiery marinade.
Coat the chicken thoroughly and marinate at least 30 minutes, or up to overnight in the fridge.
Grill over medium-high heat, or roast at 425F (220C), turning occasionally, 25-30 minutes until well charred outside and cooked through.
Piri piri chicken traveled from Portuguese colonial Mozambique and Angola back to Portugal, becoming a beloved national dish.

name: Portuguese Bacalhau a Gomes de Sa
steps: Salt the fish and let sit 15 minutes, then poach until just cooked and flake it.
Layer the sliced potato, onion, and flaked fish in a baking dish with a drizzle of oil.
Bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until the potatoes are tender, and top with the sliced egg.
steps_detailed: Salt the fish generously and let it sit 15-20 minutes, then poach gently until just cooked through, and flake into large pieces.
Boil the sliced potato until just tender, about 10 minutes, and drain.
Saute the onion and garlic in half the oil until soft.
Layer the potato, sauteed onion, and flaked fish in a baking dish, drizzling with the remaining oil between layers.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 20-25 minutes until heated through and lightly golden on top.
Top with the sliced hard-boiled egg and olives before serving - one of Portugal's countless celebrated bacalhau preparations.

name: Portuguese Francesinha
steps: Cook the beef patty and warm the ham.
Layer the meats and cheese between the bread.
Melt more cheese over the top under the broiler, and serve with a simple tomato-beer sauce if you have one.
steps_detailed: Cook the beef patty until browned and cooked through, and warm the ham in the same pan.
Layer the beef, ham, and half the cheese between two slices of the bread, then top with the remaining cheese.
Simmer the diced tomatoes, beef broth, and mustard into a thick sauce.
Broil the sandwich briefly until the cheese is fully melted and bubbling, then pour the hot sauce over the top.
A rich, indulgent sandwich from Porto, inspired by the French croque monsieur but built into something much bigger.

name: Portuguese Frango Assado
steps: Combine the garlic, lime juice, and oil into a marinade, and coat the chicken.
Marinate at least 20 minutes, then roast at 425F for 25-30 minutes until well browned and cooked through.
Serve with fries and a simple salad.
steps_detailed: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, oregano, and black pepper into a marinade, and coat the chicken thoroughly.
Marinate at least 30 minutes, or overnight in the fridge for deeper flavor.
Roast at 425F (220C) for 28-32 minutes, or grill, until well browned and cooked through, basting once with the marinade partway through.
Serve with fries and a simple salad - Portuguese-style roast chicken, a favorite at churrasqueira grill houses.

name: Portuguese Pasteis de Bacalhau
steps: Salt the fish and let sit, then poach and flake it finely.
Mix the flaked fish with the mashed potato and egg.
Shape into small ovals with two spoons and fry until golden on all sides.
steps_detailed: Salt the fish generously and let it sit 15-20 minutes, then poach gently until just cooked, and flake it very finely.
Mix the flaked fish with the mashed potato, egg, onion, and cilantro until well combined.
Shape into small ovals using two spoons (the traditional quenelle shape).
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat and fry the fritters, turning occasionally, 4-5 minutes until golden on all sides.
Drain briefly and serve warm - a beloved Portuguese bar snack and appetizer.

name: Portuguese Chourico Bites

ingredients.note: 1.5 servings, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps: Slice the sausage into thick rounds.
Fry in the oil until browned and slightly crisp at the edges, about 5-6 minutes.
Serve warm as a simple snack.

ingredients_full.note: 2 servings, sliced, as a chourico stand-in

steps_detailed: Slice the sausage into thick rounds.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 5-7 minutes, until well browned and slightly crisp at the edges, letting some of the paprika-red fat render.
Finish with a squeeze of lime and serve warm with toothpicks - simple, smoky, and a fixture at Portuguese gatherings.

name: Portuguese Rissois
steps: Cook the flour and milk together over low heat, stirring constantly, into a thick, smooth paste dough.
Mix in the chopped shrimp.
Shape into small half-moons, coat lightly in flour, and fry until golden.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter in a saucepan, whisk in half the flour, and cook 1 minute, then gradually whisk in the milk until smooth.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, 5-6 minutes until it forms a very thick, smooth dough that pulls away from the pan sides. Let cool.
Stir the chopped shrimp into the cooled dough.
Roll out and cut into rounds, fold into half-moons, and seal the edges, then dip in the beaten egg and the remaining flour.
Heat the oil in a skillet and fry 2-3 minutes per side until golden - a beloved Portuguese pastry snack, similar to a shrimp-filled turnover.

name: Portuguese Bifana
steps: Season the pork with the garlic and simmer or pan-fry in a little liquid until very tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Warm the bread.
Pile the pork into the bread and finish with a swipe of mustard.
steps_detailed: Season the thinly sliced pork with the garlic, vinegar, black pepper, and bay leaf, and let marinate at least 20 minutes.
Simmer the pork in a splash of water with its marinade for 15-20 minutes until very tender, then remove the bay leaf.
Warm the bread roll and split it.
Pile the tender pork into the roll, spoon a little of the cooking liquid over it, and finish with mustard - a beloved Portuguese sandwich, found at cafes throughout the country.

name: Portuguese Arroz Doce
steps: Simmer the rice in water until half-cooked, about 8 minutes, then drain.
Add the milk and sugar, and continue simmering 15-18 minutes until thick and creamy.
Dust with cinnamon before serving.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in 1 cup water for 8-10 minutes until about half-cooked, then drain.
Add the milk and sugar, and continue simmering over low heat, stirring often, 18-22 minutes until the rice is fully tender and the mixture is thick and creamy.
Whisk a spoonful of the hot rice mixture into the egg yolk to temper it, then stir it back into the pot and cook 1-2 more minutes until slightly thickened.
Pour into bowls and dust generously with cinnamon, often in a decorative pattern - Portugal's beloved rice pudding, found at nearly every celebration.

name: Portuguese Bolo de Bolacha

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps: Cream the butter and sugar together until light.
Dip the biscuits briefly in the milk and layer them with the butter mixture in a small dish, building up layers.
Chill at least an hour before slicing.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps_detailed: Cream the butter, sugar, and vanilla together until light and fluffy.
Briefly dip each biscuit in the milk (just a quick dip, not a soak, so they stay firm) and layer them in a small dish, spreading a thin layer of the butter mixture between each layer of biscuits.
Finish with a layer of the butter mixture on top.
Chill at least 1-2 hours, or overnight, until firm, then slice - a beloved no-bake Portuguese icebox cake, found in nearly every home.

name: Portuguese Pao de Lo
steps: Beat the eggs and sugar together until very pale, thick, and roughly tripled in volume, about 5-6 minutes.
Gently fold in the flour in two additions, keeping as much air in the batter as possible.
Pour into a small buttered pan and bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until just set and springy.
steps_detailed: Beat the eggs, sugar, and vanilla together vigorously for 6-8 minutes until very pale, thick, and roughly tripled in volume - this is the key step, since the cake relies entirely on the air beaten into the eggs.
Sift the flour over the mixture and fold it in gently in two or three additions, being careful not to deflate the batter.
Pour into a small buttered and lightly floured pan.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 20-28 minutes until just set and springy to the touch - the center is traditionally left slightly moist and custardy in some regional versions.
Let cool before slicing - Portugal's simple, beloved sponge cake, made with just eggs, sugar, and flour.

name: Portuguese Piri Piri Sauce

ingredients.note: or piri piri pepper if available

steps: Blend the peppers, garlic, lime juice, and oil into a smooth, fiery sauce.
Season with salt.
Use as a marinade or table condiment for grilled meats.

ingredients_full.note: or piri piri pepper if available

steps_detailed: Combine the peppers, garlic, lime juice, oil, and oregano in a blender and blend until smooth.
Season with salt to taste.
Store in a sealed jar in the fridge for up to two weeks - use as a marinade, table condiment, or basting sauce for grilled meats.

name: Portuguese Molho Verde
steps: Combine the cilantro, garlic, oil, and vinegar.
Season with salt and let sit 10 minutes before serving over grilled fish or meat.
steps_detailed: Finely chop the cilantro (or parsley, both common in Portuguese green sauces) and combine with the garlic, oil, vinegar, and lime juice.
Season generously with salt and let sit at room temperature 10-15 minutes so the flavors meld.
Spoon over grilled fish, meat, or boiled potatoes.
